other_observers: Andrew Core and Sean Walters who agreed with identification
description: Petrochelidon swallow hunting low over mud/sandflats with a goodly number (50-100) Cliff Swallows and a similar number of Bank Swallows. Bird first seen when flying straight towards me, making plain orange throat apparent, at which time I drew my companions' attention to the bird.  Shape much like that of Cliff Swallow. Head/neck: Small orange forehead patch that was considerably darker than that of adult (Colorado) Cliff Swallow, but probably within range of young Cliff Swallows. Size of forehead patch consistent with immature of either species. Throat was a medium orange that was distinctly paler than that of any Cliff Swallow present and was unmarred by dark (or light) markings. This orange went up onto auriculars and bled into collar which was grayish on hindneck. The demarcation between color and back was fairly crisp. Crown dull dark glossy blue, barely extending down to eye and curving sharply upwards posterior to eye. Upperparts: Back and wings dull glossy dark blue, without white streaking. Rump orange. Tail dark and square. Underparts: Chest white. Sides orange-buff mixed with dusky. Undertail coverts washed in orange-buff and with very limited markings, seemingly limited a couple narrow dusky smudges on outermost undertail covert feathers, and those only posteriorly. Note that small area of yellow at gape noted.Behavior: See DescriptionCall: Not heardPlumage: immature
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
